coordSystem.note: Band A and Band B follow the official Vatican Museums scheme image row orientation and should be mapped to your real wall constants in one adapter.

panels.title: Drunkenness of Noah
panels.description: Noah tills the soil and is shown drunk after the Flood.
panels.narration: Noah's post-Flood drunkenness closes the Noah cycle at the entrance end.

panels.title: The Flood
panels.description: A crowded scene of catastrophe, refuge, and the saving ark.
panels.narration: People struggle for survival while the ark remains the instrument of salvation.

panels.title: Sacrifice of Noah
panels.description: Noah's family gives thanks after surviving the Flood.
panels.narration: This sacrifice marks the rebirth of humanity after destruction.

panels.title: Original Sin and Banishment
panels.description: Temptation and expulsion are fused into one panel.
panels.narration: Michelangelo joins the sin and its consequence into a single image.
panels.ariaLabel: Ceiling panel, Original Sin and Banishment

panels.title: Creation of Eve
panels.description: Eve rises in the central Adam and Eve triad.
panels.narration: The centre of the narrative sequence turns to the creation of woman.

panels.title: Creation of Adam
panels.description: God reaches toward Adam to give life.
panels.narration: This is the best-known image in the whole cycle and deserves a hero zoom profile.

panels.title: Separation of Land and Sea
panels.description: God orders the waters and the earth.
panels.narration: The creation cycle shifts from humanity back to cosmological order.
panels.ariaLabel: Ceiling panel, Separation of Land and Sea

panels.title: Creation of the Sun, Moon and Plants
panels.description: Vegetation and the celestial lights are created in one panel.
panels.narration: Michelangelo shows the third and fourth day together, with two divine actions in one image.
panels.ariaLabel: Ceiling panel, Creation of the Sun, Moon and Plants

panels.title: Separation of Light from Darkness
panels.description: God divides light from darkness at the altar end.
panels.narration: The altar-end scene crowns the creation sequence in physical space.
panels.ariaLabel: Ceiling panel, Separation of Light from Darkness

panels.title: Zechariah
panels.description: Entrance-end prophet above the ceremonial threshold.
panels.narration: Zechariah anchors the entrance end of the prophetic ring.

panels.title: Joel
panels.description: First long-side prophet from the entrance on Band A.
panels.narration: Joel begins one alternating sequence of prophets and sibyls.

panels.title: Erythraean Sibyl
panels.description: Second figure in the Band A alternating sequence.
panels.narration: The Erythraean Sibyl extends prophecy beyond Israel.

panels.title: Ezekiel
panels.description: Middle prophet on Band A.
panels.narration: Ezekiel sits among the monumental throne figures circling the Genesis strip.

panels.title: Persian Sibyl
panels.description: Penultimate Band A sibyl close to the altar half.
panels.narration: The Persian Sibyl occupies the later part of the prophetic circuit.

panels.title: Jeremiah
panels.description: Band A prophet nearest Jonah.
panels.narration: Jeremiah closes one long-side sequence before the altar-end figure.

panels.title: Jonah
panels.description: Altar-end prophet prefiguring Christ.
panels.narration: Jonah dominates the altar end and functions as the strongest Christological signal among the vigils.

panels.title: Libyan Sibyl
panels.description: Band B sibyl nearest the altar end.
panels.narration: The Libyan Sibyl begins the return side of the prophetic ring.

panels.title: Daniel
panels.description: Second from the altar on Band B in the standard arrangement.
panels.narration: Daniel sits reading, turning the viewer back from the altar toward the entrance end.

panels.title: Cumaean Sibyl
panels.description: Middle sibyl on Band B.
panels.narration: The Cumaean Sibyl balances the prophetic sequence opposite Ezekiel.

panels.title: Isaiah
panels.description: Entrance-half prophet on Band B.
panels.narration: Isaiah belongs to the earlier, entrance-half portion of the prophetic ring.

panels.title: Delphic Sibyl
panels.description: First entrance-side sibyl on Band B.
panels.narration: The Delphic Sibyl begins the Band B sequence close to Zechariah at the entrance end.

panels.title: Severy of the Azor and Zadok Bay
panels.description: Triangular severy above the Azor and Zadok lunette, by convention associated with that ancestral bay.
panels.narration: An ancestor group in the genealogy of Christ. Wikipedia notes the figures in the triangular severies have not been positively identified, so this name follows the adjacent lunette bay.
panels.ariaLabel: Ancestor severy, Azor and Zadok bay

panels.title: Severy of the Josiah Bay
panels.description: Triangular severy above the Josiah, Jeconiah and Shealtiel lunette, by convention associated with that ancestral bay.
panels.narration: An ancestor group in the genealogy of Christ. Wikipedia notes the figures in the triangular severies have not been positively identified, so this name follows the adjacent lunette bay.

panels.title: Severy of the Rehoboam Bay
panels.description: Triangular severy above the Rehoboam and Abijah lunette, by convention associated with that ancestral bay.
panels.narration: An ancestor group in the genealogy of Christ. Wikipedia notes the figures in the triangular severies have not been positively identified, so this name follows the adjacent lunette bay.

panels.title: Severy of the Salmon Bay
panels.description: Triangular severy above the Salmon, Boaz and Obed lunette, by convention associated with that ancestral bay.
panels.narration: An ancestor group in the genealogy of Christ. Wikipedia notes the figures in the triangular severies have not been positively identified, so this name follows the adjacent lunette bay.

panels.title: Severy of the Achim Bay
panels.description: Triangular severy above the Achim and Eliud lunette, by convention associated with that ancestral bay.
panels.narration: An ancestor group in the genealogy of Christ. Wikipedia notes the figures in the triangular severies have not been positively identified, so this name follows the adjacent lunette bay.

panels.title: Severy of the Zerubbabel Bay
panels.description: Triangular severy above the Zerubbabel, Abiud and Eliakim lunette, by convention associated with that ancestral bay.
panels.narration: An ancestor group in the genealogy of Christ. Wikipedia notes the figures in the triangular severies have not been positively identified, so this name follows the adjacent lunette bay.

panels.title: Severy of the Asa Bay
panels.description: Triangular severy above the Asa, Jehoshaphat and Joram lunette, by convention associated with that ancestral bay.
panels.narration: An ancestor group in the genealogy of Christ. Wikipedia notes the figures in the triangular severies have not been positively identified, so this name follows the adjacent lunette bay.

panels.title: Severy of the Jesse Bay
panels.description: Triangular severy above the Jesse, David and Solomon lunette, by convention associated with that ancestral bay.
panels.narration: An ancestor group in the genealogy of Christ. Wikipedia notes the figures in the triangular severies have not been positively identified, so this name follows the adjacent lunette bay.

panels.title: Judith and Holofernes
panels.description: Judith escapes with the head of Holofernes.
panels.narration: One of the four salvation scenes at the corners of the vault.

panels.title: David and Goliath
panels.description: David overcomes Goliath in the entrance-end corner zone.
panels.narration: This corner scene is one of the strongest typological victory images.

panels.title: Punishment of Haman
panels.description: Haman is executed in a multi-moment salvation image.
panels.narration: The official corner-subject set places Haman at the altar end.

panels.title: Brazen Serpent
panels.description: The afflicted turn toward the brazen serpent for healing.
panels.narration: This crowded corner image balances Haman on the altar side.

panels.title: Aminadab
panels.description: Lunette tablet inscribed with the ancestor Aminadab, with a family group beside it.
panels.narration: Above a window, this lunette names Aminadab in the genealogy of Christ running from Abraham to Joseph (Gospel of Matthew, chapter 1).

panels.title: Naason
panels.description: Lunette tablet inscribed with the ancestor Naason, with a family group beside it.
panels.narration: Above a window, this lunette names Naason in the genealogy of Christ running from Abraham to Joseph (Gospel of Matthew, chapter 1).

panels.title: Salmon, Boaz and Obed
panels.description: Lunette tablet inscribed with the ancestors Salmon, Boaz and Obed, with a family group beside it.
panels.narration: Above a window, this lunette names Salmon, Boaz and Obed in the genealogy of Christ from Abraham to Joseph (Gospel of Matthew, chapter 1).

panels.title: Jesse, David and Solomon
panels.description: Lunette tablet inscribed with the ancestors Jesse, David and Solomon, with a family group beside it.
panels.narration: Above a window, this lunette names Jesse, David and Solomon in the genealogy of Christ from Abraham to Joseph (Gospel of Matthew, chapter 1).

panels.title: Rehoboam and Abijah
panels.description: Lunette tablet inscribed with the ancestors Rehoboam and Abijah, with a family group beside it.
panels.narration: Above a window, this lunette names Rehoboam and Abijah in the genealogy of Christ from Abraham to Joseph (Gospel of Matthew, chapter 1).

panels.title: Asa, Jehoshaphat and Joram
panels.description: Lunette tablet inscribed with the ancestors Asa, Jehoshaphat and Joram, with a family group beside it.
panels.narration: Above a window, this lunette names Asa, Jehoshaphat and Joram in the genealogy of Christ from Abraham to Joseph (Gospel of Matthew, chapter 1).

panels.title: Uzziah, Jotham and Ahaz
panels.description: Lunette tablet inscribed with the ancestors Uzziah, Jotham and Ahaz, with a family group beside it.
panels.narration: Above a window, this lunette names Uzziah, Jotham and Ahaz in the genealogy of Christ from Abraham to Joseph (Gospel of Matthew, chapter 1).

panels.title: Hezekiah, Manasseh and Amon
panels.description: Lunette tablet inscribed with the ancestors Hezekiah, Manasseh and Amon, with a family group beside it.
panels.narration: Above a window, this lunette names Hezekiah, Manasseh and Amon in the genealogy of Christ from Abraham to Joseph (Gospel of Matthew, chapter 1).

panels.title: Josiah, Jeconiah and Shealtiel
panels.description: Lunette tablet inscribed with the ancestors Josiah, Jeconiah and Shealtiel, with a family group beside it.
panels.narration: Above a window, this lunette names Josiah, Jeconiah and Shealtiel in the genealogy of Christ from Abraham to Joseph (Gospel of Matthew, chapter 1).

panels.title: Zerubbabel, Abiud and Eliakim
panels.description: Lunette tablet inscribed with the ancestors Zerubbabel, Abiud and Eliakim, with a family group beside it.
panels.narration: Above a window, this lunette names Zerubbabel, Abiud and Eliakim in the genealogy of Christ from Abraham to Joseph (Gospel of Matthew, chapter 1).

panels.title: Azor and Zadok
panels.description: Lunette tablet inscribed with the ancestors Azor and Zadok, with a family group beside it.
panels.narration: Above a window, this lunette names Azor and Zadok in the genealogy of Christ from Abraham to Joseph (Gospel of Matthew, chapter 1).

panels.title: Achim and Eliud
panels.description: Lunette tablet inscribed with the ancestors Achim and Eliud, with a family group beside it.
panels.narration: Above a window, this lunette names Achim and Eliud in the genealogy of Christ from Abraham to Joseph (Gospel of Matthew, chapter 1).

panels.title: Eleazar and Matthan
panels.description: Lunette tablet inscribed with the ancestors Eleazar and Matthan, with a family group beside it.
panels.narration: Above a window, this lunette names Eleazar and Matthan in the genealogy of Christ from Abraham to Joseph (Gospel of Matthew, chapter 1).

panels.title: Jacob and Joseph
panels.description: Lunette tablet inscribed with Jacob and Joseph, the husband of Mary, ending the genealogy.
panels.narration: Above a window, this lunette names Jacob and Joseph, closing the genealogy of Christ from Abraham to Joseph (Gospel of Matthew, chapter 1).
